Defendant Anne M Disney Family Trust'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. ARASH MOUSSAUVIAN, ESQ. (Tel No. 415-695-4545) and RAVI KRISHNANEY, ESQ. of R. Krishnaney & Company APC (Tel. 415-669-4463) appearing for the Plaintiff (Mr. Moussauvian specially appearing);
Atty. STEVIE B. NEWTON, ESQ. of Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane LLP, Tel No. 415-981-5411 appearing for the Defendant Anne M. Disney Family Trust by James H. Disney.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's demurrer to the first, third, and fourth causes of action in the first amended complaint is overruled. As to the first claim for breach of oral contract, plaintiff alleged sufficient facts to invoke the equitable estoppel exception to defendant's defense based on the statute of frauds defense. The third and fourth claims for promissory estoppel and specific performance are adequately pled, not uncertain, and cognizable claims. Defendant shall have 30 days to answer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

Motion To Quash [Or Modify] Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To State Farm
Off Calendar Mar-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stevie B. Newton, Esq. of Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane LLP, Tel. 415.981.5411, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: Defendant's counsel stipualted to the Judge Pro Tem. There was no objection to the Court's tentative ruling which was adopted. DEFENDANT ANNE M DISNEY FAMILY TRUST's Motion To Quash [Or Modify] Plaintiff's Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To State Farm - Motion to quash is granted without prejudice to the subpoena being re-served. The Court finds that the proof of service of the subpoena on State Farm was not served on Defendant within 10 days of the date of the deposition as required by CCP 1985.3(b), et. seq. If the subpoena is re-served, the term "premises" must be properly defined. The Court finds that neither party facilitated communication with the other on this issue, and therefore, denies the various requests for sanctions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.
